summary: readBatchCSV distinguishes io.EOF from real CSV parse/read errors so mid-file failures do not silently truncate the batch.
reason: Generator-emitted readBatchCSV treated any csv.Reader.Read() error as terminal EOF. A malformed quoted field or OS read failure at row 50 of 1000 would return 49 rows with a nil error, then send those 49 rows as if the batch were complete -- a silent compliance and data-integrity failure for SMS workflows. Surfaced by Greptile P1 in the PR #417 review.
validated_outcome: go build ./... succeeds; go test ./... passes; go vet clean.
